s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL
REMOVE REAR SEAT CUSHION ASSEMBLY
REMOVE REAR SEAT HEADREST ASSEMBLY
REMOVE REAR CENTER SEAT HEADREST ASSEMBLY
REMOVE REAR SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY (for Fixed Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 3 rear seat belts from the 3 rear seat shoulder belt guides.
Remove the 4 bolts and rear seat back assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SEPARATE TYPE REAR SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY LH (for Fold Down Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and separate type rear seat back assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SEPARATE TYPE REAR SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY RH (for Fold Down Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and separate type rear seat back assembly RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SIDE SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY LH (for Fold Down Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and rear side seat back assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SIDE SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY RH (for Fold Down Seat Type)

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SEAT BACK COVER (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, detach the claws and remove the 2 rear seat back covers.

item.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.
Use the same procedures for the RH side and LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SEPARATE TYPE REAR SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY LH (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Separate the 2 rear seat belts.
Disengage the 3 clamps and then separate the cover.
Remove the 2 bolts and 2 nuts and separate type rear seat back assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SEPARATE TYPE REAR SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY RH (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Separate the rear seat belt.
Disengage the 3 clamps and then separate the cover.
Remove the 2 bolts and 2 nuts and separate type rear seat back assembly RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE CENTER SEAT BACK ASSEMBLY (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and center seat back assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DOOR SCUFF PLATE LH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 5 claws and 2 clips, and remove the rear door scuff plate LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DOOR OPENING TRIM WEATHERSTRIP LH
REMOVE REAR DOOR SCUFF PLATE RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DOOR OPENING TRIM WEATHERSTRIP RH
REMOVE RECLINING REMOTE CONTROL LEVER SUB-ASSEMBLY LH (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt.
Disengage the 2 clips and the claw and then separate the reclining remote control lever sub-assembly.
Separate the rear seat reclining control cable and remove the reclining remote control lever sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE RECLINING REMOTE CONTROL LEVER SUB-ASSEMBLY RH (for Reclining Seat Type)
REMOVE ROOF SIDE INNER GARNISH LH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 5 clips and 2 claws, and remove the roof side inner garnish LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF SIDE INNER GARNISH RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE CHILD RESTRAINT SEAT ANCHOR BRACKET SUB-ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 nuts and child restraint seat anchor bracket sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT REAR SEAT INNER WITH CENTER BELT ASSEMBLY LH (for TMC Made)

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the floor end of the rear seat inner with center belt assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT REAR SEAT INNER WITH CENTER BELT ASSEMBLY LH (for TMMK Made)

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and disconnect the floor end of the rear seat inner with center belt assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT REAR SEAT OUTER BELT ASSEMBLY (for LH Side)

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the floor end of the rear seat outer belt assembly.

s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT REAR SEAT OUTER BELT ASSEMBLY (for RH Side)

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and disconnect the floor end of the rear seat outer belt assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SEAT SHOULDER BELT COVER

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 4 claws and remove the rear seat shoulder belt cover.

item.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.
Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR SEAT SHOULDER BELT HOLE COVER

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 4 claws and remove the rear seat shoulder belt hole cover.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PACKAGE TRAY TRIM PANEL ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Disengage the 4 clips and the 5 claws.
Pass the 3 rear seat belt floor anchors through the package tray trim panel assembly then remove the package tray trim panel assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PACKAGE TRAY TRIM PANEL ASSEMBLY (for Reclining Seat Type)

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Disengage the 4 clips and the 5 claws.
Pass the 3 rear seat belt floor anchors through the package tray trim panel assembly then remove the package tray trim panel assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR ASSIST GRIP SUB-ASSEMBLY

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the rear side and the front side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SPOT LIGHT ASSEMBLY (w/ Sliding Roof)

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 2 claws labeled A and 2 claws labeled B from the vehicle body.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 2 claws labeled C and remove the spot light assembly from the headlining.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 4 claws.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Pull the light bulb holder in the direction indicated by the arrow in the illustration to detach the claw. Then remove the spot light assembly from the light bulb holder.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE SUNSHADE TRIM HOLDER (w/ Rear Sunshade)

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, turn the pin of each sunshade trim holder 90°.
Disengage the 2 claws and remove the sunshade trim holder.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF HEADLINING ASSEMBLY (w/o Sliding Roof)

content1.ptxt: It is not necessary to completely remove the roof headlining. Slightly lower the rear section of the roof headlining so that the back window glass can be removed (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF HEADLINING ASSEMBLY (w/ Sliding Roof)

content1.ptxt: It is not necessary to completely remove the roof headlining. Slightly lower the rear section of the roof headlining so that the back window glass can be removed (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE BACK WINDOW MOULDING

s2.ptxt: Using a knife, cut off the moulding, as shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: Do not damage the vehicle body with the knife.
If the vehicle body is damaged, repair the damaged portion by applying an anti-rust coating after removing the back window glass assembly.

s2.ptxt: Remove the remaining moulding from the back window glass.

atten4.ptxt: When removing the moulding, make a partial cut, then pull and remove it by hand.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE BACK WINDOW GLASS ASSEMBLY

item.ptxt: There are No. 1 and No. 2 stoppers on the back window glass as shown in the illustration. Be careful not to damage the back window glass when cutting off the adhesive.
To prevent the back window glass from dropping when performing this operation, be sure to hold the back window glass using suction cups.

atten4.ptxt: Depending on vehicles, a 1-piece or 2-piece type stopper is used.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Disconnect the connectors.
Apply protective tape to the installation position of the back window glass assembly on the vehicle body.

atten4.ptxt: Apply protective tape to the installation surface to prevent it from being scratched.

s2.ptxt: Pass a piano wire between the vehicle body and glass from the interior.
Tie both wire ends to wooden blocks or similar objects.
Cut off the adhesive by pulling the piano wire around the back window glass.

item.ptxt: When separating the back window glass, take care not to damage the paint or the interior and exterior ornaments.
To prevent the package tray trim panel from being scratched when removing the back window glass, place a plastic sheet between the piano wire and package tray trim panel.

s2.ptxt: Place matchmarks on the back window glass and vehicle body on the locations indicated in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Matchmarks do not need to be placed if the back window glass is not going to be reused.

s2.ptxt: Using a suction cup, remove the back window glass assembly.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to drop the back window glass.
Leave as much adhesive on the vehicle body as possible when removing the back window glass.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 BACK WINDOW GLASS STOPPER

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the 2 No. 2 stoppers.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the back window glass.
Be sure to replace the No. 2 stoppers with new ones.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 BACK WINDOW GLASS STOPPER (for 2-piece Type)

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the 2 No. 1 stoppers.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to replace the No. 1 stoppers with new ones.

s-1.ptxt: CLEAN BACK WINDOW GLASS

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the adhesive tape and adhesive sticking to the back window glass.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the windshield glass.

s2.ptxt: Clean the outer circumference of the back window glass with a non-residue solvent.

item.ptxt: Do not touch the back window glass surface after cleaning it.
Even if using a new back window glass, clean the back window glass with a non-residue solvent.

s-1.ptxt: CLEAN VEHICLE BODY

s2.ptxt: Clean and shape the contact surface of the vehicle body.

s3.ptxt: Using a knife, cut away any rough adhesive on the contact surface of the vehicle body to ensure the appropriate surface shape.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the vehicle body.

atten4.ptxt: Leave as much adhesive on the vehicle body as possible.

s3.ptxt: Clean the contact surface of the vehicle body with a piece of cloth saturated with cleaner.

atten4.ptxt: Even if all the adhesive has been removed, clean the vehicle body.
